2022-11-21

javax.annotation.Generated

javax.annotation.Generated is a Java annotation type that can be used to indicate that an element was generated. It is part of the javax.annotation package, and was introduced in version 1.5 of Java. The purpose of this annotation is to indicate that a particular element, such as a field, method, or class, was generated by a particular tool or process. This information may be useful to developers who are debugging a program, or as a guide to understanding the codebase better.

Syntax

The syntax for declaring a javax.annotation.Generated annotation is as follows: 

          
           
@Generated(value={tool name}, date=YYYY-MM-DD, comments={comments})
The value argument must be a string that contains the name of the tool or process that generated the element. The date argument is a date string in the format "YYYY-MM-DD" that indicates when the element was generated. The comments argument is an optional string containing any additional comments about the tool or process that generated the element.

Example

Here is an example of a class with a javax.annotation.Generated annotation: 

          
           
@Generated(value="MyTool", date="2021-04-02", comments="Generates Java classes")
public class MyClass {
  // ...
}
This annotation indicates that the class was generated by the tool called "MyTool" on April 2nd, 2021, and provides a comment about the purpose of the tool.
